name: Patient C
presenting_complaint: Sudden severe abdominal and lower back pain 40 minutes ago; brought in by private ambulance after collapsing at home.
history: Long-standing hypertension, ex-smoker (50 pack-year), known abdominal aortic aneurysm 5.4cm under surveillance. Pain is 9/10 ripping/tearing into the back. Diaphoretic. AVPU=A but visibly mottled. Wife is at bedside, anxious but cooperative.

trajectory.state: Pale, diaphoretic, in obvious pain 9/10. SBP 92, HR 112, mottled lower limbs. Pulsatile abdominal mass palpable.
Two large-bore IVs needed; SBP drifts to 84 despite 500mL crystalloid. Massive transfusion protocol should be initiated. Vascular surgery + ICU + interventional radiology must all be paged.
Brief loss of consciousness during bedside ultrasound. SBP 70, HR 130. Permissive hypotension target SBP 80-90. Theatre or hybrid IR suite must be prepared.
If transferred to OR with vascular team and blood products: viable. If delayed for non-urgent imaging or single-consultant indecision: cardiac arrest.

confirmatory_tests: CT angiogram aorta (only if haemodynamically borderline-stable)
Group and crossmatch x6 units, activate massive transfusion protocol
Vascular surgery + anaesthetics + interventional radiology consults
red_herrings: Pain initially attributed to 'renal colic' by paramedic handover
Brief transient SBP rise after fluid bolus may falsely reassure
